sourceMovie.title: Hellboy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Hellboy (2004) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hellboy clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Hellboy clicked for you, Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Hellboy?
faq.answer: Try The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring, Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness, Batman—each shares DNA with Hellboy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Hellboy?
faq.answer: Hellboy (2004) is best described as Fantasy, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Hellboy part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Hellboy Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
